LAW AND MOTION 301,RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. "[T]HE COURT RETAINS AUTHORITY TO DISMISS AN ACTION IF AN UNREPRESENTED CORPORATION DOES NOT OBTAIN COUNSEL WITHIN REASONABLE TIME." (CLD CONSTRUCTION, INC. V. CITY OF SAN RAMON (2004) 120 CAL.APP.4TH 1141, 1150.) THE COURT CONTINUES THIS MATTER ON ITS OWN MOTION TO MAY 13, 2025. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/CVA)

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Dec-31-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendant Tantalus Builders Inc.

Dept. 505; Judge Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:31 am and concluding at 9:33 am

Plaintiff Bonelli Enterprises LLC is represented by Mac Cabal, Esq.
Defendant Tantalus Builders Inc. not appearing.
Proof of service on file.

ANDREW BONDE sworn to testify on behalf of plaintiff.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Bonelli Enterprises LLC, and against Defendant Tantalus Builders Inc.

Plaintiff Bonelli Enterprises LLC, is awarded judgment against Defendant Tantalus Builders Inc. as follows:

Damages/Principal: $72,909.87
Pre-Judgment Interest: $50,570.10
Costs: $3,173.40
Attorney Fees: $47,760.00
Total Judgment: $174,413.37

Proposed Judgment was submitted and signed by the Court. (D505)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: CROSS DEFENDANT BONELLI ENTERPRISES, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Bonelli Enterprises, LLC's motion to strike Tantalus Builders, Inc.'s answer and cross-complaint in its entirety is granted. Tantalus has not obtained counsel and may not appear in the court through a non-attorney. "[T]he court retains authority to dismiss an action if an unrepresented corporation does not obtain counsel within reasonable time." (CLD Construction, Inc. v. City of San Ramon (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1141, 1150.) Tantalus has been on notice of its need to obtain counsel since at least February 2025 and has not yet done so.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-02-2025 Continued To May-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: RE: CROSS DEFENDANT BONELLI ENTERPRISES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling. "[T]he court retains authority to dismiss an action if an unrepresented corporation does not obtain counsel within reasonable time." (CLD Construction, Inc. v. City of San Ramon (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1141, 1150.) Here, Tantalus Builders, Inc., has been on notice of the need to secure counsel since at least February 18, 2025. Trial is set for July 7, 2025. Moving party Bonelli Enterprises should not be required to wait until the eve of trial to learn whether Tantalus has secured representation. The court continues this matter on its own motion to May 13, 2025 to give Tantalus a final opportunity to obtain counsel and anticipates granting the requested relief if that has not occurred.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/CVA)
